head: c1657d396
summary: Cross-cutting audit on compile-llm-notes-per-action. Pipeline (tester v2 PASS 3 fresh-cache rounds, security v1 PASS) holds on independent verification. All 8 architect stages delivered. Both verification checks reproduced independently: Compile.llm = 14905 bytes (< 16 KB bound, was ~20.8 KB); drift cases 2/2 across 3 fresh-cache plang builds (9.6s/10.4s/9.7s). C# suite 2945/2945 on clean rebuild. One new low/informational finding (F1) — orphan-scan mechanism (ScanOrphans + WarnOrphansAsync) shipped but never invoked from production code path; Stage 7 half-done (testable but doesn't actually warn on a real build). Fell in the seam between per-axis bots (tester verified by direct call, security audited mechanism safety, neither asked 'is it invoked?'). Non-blocking. A second finding (F2) was filed re: CLAUDE.md [Provider] references but retracted on rebase — docs commit 65538f9dc landed concurrently and closed it; auditor process gap (didn't git fetch before reporting). Carry-forwards from prior branches unchanged.

previous_reviews_assessed.tester: agree — v2 PASS holds. The 3-fresh-cache rule reproduces (Round1/Round2/Round3 all Pass/Pass). The non-blocking 'LLM shape variation' observation v2 flagged is the right call to file as info-not-finding. The broad plang suite's 6 fixture failures (_fixtures_fail/_fixtures_sensitive) are intentional negative-path test infrastructure, pre-existing on parent — not regressions.
previous_reviews_assessed.security: agree — v1 PASS. Trust-boundary analysis correct: moduleName/actionName come from compile-time reflection, modulesRoot from App.OsDirectory; no PLang-program-reachable input. Attribute deletions are documentation prose only, no behavior/auth/signing surface changed. The mechanism is safe. Scope correctly stopped at 'is this safe' — invocation-coverage (F1) is the auditor's seam.

findings.issue: WarnOrphansAsync exists and the mechanism is sound, but no production code path invokes it. Searching `WarnOrphansAsync|ScanOrphans` across PLang/ and PlangConsole/ returns only the definition sites and tests under PLang.Tests/App/Modules/CatalogTests/MarkdownTeachingOrphanTests.cs. Architect Stage 7: 'At catalog load, fail loud on orphan markdown files. One clear warning per orphan at startup.' Today 0 orphans exist (verified — every stem under os/system/modules/**/*.{notes,examples,description}.md maps to a registered action), so observable impact is zero. Risk is latent: a future rename, typo, or removed action with leftover .md will not produce the warning the stage promised.
findings.missed_by: tester (verified function works when called), security (verified mechanism is safe), coder (marked Stage 7 done on mechanism existence)
findings.impact: Latent — zero observable today. Next orphan introduced by a refactor will silently fail to warn, defeating the stated purpose of Stage 7 (replacement for compile-time catch of attribute typos).
findings.suggestion: Wire `await Modules.WarnOrphansAsync(actor)` into builder startup (after module registration, before first Describe()) or fold into the first Describe() call with a once-per-process guard. ~5 lines. Non-blocking.

findings.issue: Initially filed: CLAUDE.md lines 38 + 42 still referenced [Provider] / Emission/Property/Provider after Stage 8 source-side rename. Retracted on rebase — docs commit 65538f9dc landed concurrently (01:14 UTC) and closed it; CLAUDE.md now reads [Code] / Emission/Property/{Data,Code}/ at lines 39 + 43. The docs pass picked up the rename from the docs queue without an explicit claude-md-proposal from this branch.
findings.missed_by: the auditor (this bot) — didn't git fetch before reporting; committed on top of a stale local branch and only discovered the resolution when push was rejected
findings.impact: Zero. The drift was real at the moment I started auditing but closed before my commit landed. Filing as a process note rather than silently dropping the finding.
findings.suggestion: Auditor protocol: git fetch + check for concurrent commits before filing the report.

carry_forwards.note: HttpPath.Raw preserves userinfo (PLang/app/types/path/http/this.cs:94) — file not in this branch's diff

build.warnings: ~456 pre-existing generator nullable warnings, unchanged from prior bots

plang_drift_3_rounds.command: for i in 1 2 3; do rm -rf Tests/Simple/.build; plang build '--build={"files":["Tests/Simple/Start.goal"],"cache":false}'; cd Tests && plang --test Builder/CompileLlmNotes; done

stage_delivery.stage_2_renderer: verified — stepActionDetails.template gates blocks on planStep.actions
stage_delivery.stage_3_migration: verified — zero class-level [Description] / [Example] on action handlers; one property-level [Description] (latent, unused) on goal/return.cs:14
stage_delivery.stage_4_notes_authored: verified — 184 teaching files under os/system/modules/**/*.{notes,examples,description}.md; assert/module.notes.md present
stage_delivery.stage_6_verification: verified independently — see plang_drift_3_rounds and compile_llm_size_bytes above
stage_delivery.stage_7_orphan_validation: PARTIAL — see F1 (mechanism shipped, not invoked in production)
stage_delivery.stage_8_provider_to_code_rename: PARTIAL — see F2 (source-side complete, CLAUDE.md stale)
